objections: OBJ-1 BLOCKER AC-C2-02 test-writer.md trivial-pass: must_contain_all literals all already present in current schema block (80-127); drift at procedural lines 139/181/183/198/216 unenforced
OBJ-2 MAJOR AC-C2-02 qa.md brittle tasks[] sentinel: bracket pair could be inserted in unrelated prose without wiring Option-(b) consumption
OBJ-3 MAJOR AC-C2-01 prose-vs-machine drift: prose says six entries plus four stream stubs and tmp but contains_all lists only 5

addresses: OBJ-1: AC-C2-02 agents/test-writer.md sub-check now uses must_not_contain (verbatim drift sentences from current lines 139/181/183/198/216 — five strings the Procedure section emits TODAY) + positive must_contain_all literals that force Procedure-section rewrite ('per-task manifest', 'root index'). The check FAILS today because the must_not_contain literals match the current file; Dev cannot pass without rewriting the procedural sentences.

OBJ-2: AC-C2-02 agents/qa.md sub-check now uses must_not_contain on the current line 995 unscoped pytest invocation AND the codex F4 root-iteration anti-pattern ('Read tests/generated/manifest.json. For every active_tests[] entry') + positive must_contain_all on scoped pytest, root_index_path, per_task_manifest_path, task_id, current task_id literals (codex F4 + F6 schema fields).

codex iter2 finding 1 sustained as part of OBJ-1: added line 216 checklist drift to must_not_contain ('manifest.json `active_tests[].hook_check`'); added positive procedural literals.
codex iter2 finding 2 sustained as part of OBJ-2: added 'Read tests/generated/manifest.json. For every active_tests[] entry' to must_not_contain (the current qa.md:994 line begin).

codex iter2 finding 3 sustained as NEW commands/dev.md sub-check (Section 7 P1.2 lockstep): must_not_contain current line 632 verification wording + must_contain_all three artifacts (root index + per-task manifest + report); spec Section 7 P1.2 explicitly says 'in lockstep'.
codex iter2 finding 4 sustained: AC-C2-01 allowed_write_path_prefixes match=set_equals enforces Section 7 'limited to'.

meta.ac_uid_algorithm: sha256(type + given + when + then + json.dumps(check, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':'), ensure_ascii=False))[:16]
meta.cycle_1_acs_intentionally_omitted: The 18 acceptance criteria from Cycle 1 (acceptance-criteria-20260519-132417.json) were verified PASS by QA Step 11. Cycle 2 dev MUST NOT regress them. Re-listing them here would invite re-verification noise; QA Cycle 2 runs the Cycle-2-specific ACs below AND spot-checks that Cycle 1's smoke-test outputs (recorded in spec Section 3) still hold.
codex_findings_incorporated: P1.2 QA scoped to current task_id
iter2 codex F1 added line 216 checklist drift to AC-C2-02 test-writer.md must_not_contain; positive must_contain_all gains 'per-task manifest' + 'root index' procedural literals
iter2 codex F2 added 'Read tests/generated/manifest.json. For every active_tests[] entry' to AC-C2-02 qa.md must_not_contain (codex F4 anti-pattern)
iter2 codex F3 new commands/dev.md sub-check (Section 7 P1.2 lockstep): must_not_contain line 632 wording + must_contain_all on root index + per-task manifest + report + 'all three'
iter2 codex F4 changed AC-C2-01 allowed_write_path_prefixes match from contains_all to set_equals (enforces Section 7 P1.1 'limited to')

items.when: the JSON is parsed and roles['test-writer'] is read
items.then: the role exists; allowed_tools contains exactly [Read,Glob,Grep,Bash,Write,Edit,MultiEdit] (set equality, order not required); allowed_write_path_prefixes EQUALS EXACTLY the five Section 7 P1.1 verbatim entries (*/tests/generated/, */docs/dev/test-writer-report-, */.claude/specs/*/cp-state-test-writer.json, */docs/dev/specs/*/cp-state-test-writer.json, */.claude/dev-registry/*/test-writer.json) — Section 7 wording is 'Write/Edit limited to tests/generated/** + its registry/cp-state paths', so set_equals enforces 'limited to'; denied_write_path_prefixes contains the protected-dir set (/src/, /app/, /lib/, /.claude/hooks/, /.claude/agents/, /.claude/commands/, /.claude/policies/, /.claude/schemas/, /.claude/scripts/, /.claude/settings.json) and DOES NOT contain /tests/ (codex F1: /tests/ would block the role's own purpose)
items.ac_uid_regenerated_reason: OBJ-3 iter2 — match changed from contains_all to set_equals; prose tightened to Section 7 P1.1 verbatim (no stream stubs and tmp)

items.given: agents/test-writer.md, agents/qa.md, and commands/dev.md after Cycle 2 dev edits
items.when: each file is read and the manifest-contract sentences are inspected
items.then: all three files describe Option (b) consistently AND the current procedural drift is REMOVED: (i) test-writer.md no longer contains the four verbatim drifted sentences from lines 139/181/183/198 nor the line 216 checklist phrasing referring to root manifest active_tests[]; the Procedure section now describes tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json as the per-task active manifest AND tests/generated/manifest.json as the root index (codex F3); (ii) qa.md Phase 5 no longer iterates root active_tests[] directly nor runs unscoped pytest tests/generated/ -q (line 995); Phase 5 reads root_index_path to find current task_id, then per_task_manifest_path, then runs pytest scoped to tests/generated/<task_id>/ (codex F4 + F6); (iii) commands/dev.md verification step at the former line 632 no longer asserts only the root manifest + report; it now requires all three artifacts: root index, per-task manifest, and report (codex F5 lockstep)

files_and_patterns.must_not_contain_rationale: five literals are verbatim from the CURRENT test-writer.md drifted sentences: line 139 (Check existing manifest** at `tests/generated/manifest.json`), line 181 (Read existing `tests/generated/manifest.json` if present), line 183 (Write `tests/generated/manifest.json`), line 198 ("manifest_path": "tests/generated/manifest.json"), line 216 (manifest.json `active_tests[].hook_check`). The check FAILS today because grep finds all five in the current file; Dev must rewrite the Procedure (lines 174-186), Output JSON Report (lines 191-209), and Quality Checklist (lines 213-221) sections to refer to per-task manifest as active and root manifest as index.
files_and_patterns.must_contain_all_rationale: literals 1-3 are already present in the schema block (lines 80-127, which is correct Option-b) so Dev cannot delete them; literals 4-5 ('per-task manifest', 'root index') are wording Dev MUST add to the Procedure section as part of the rewrite. The check is non-trivial because removing the must_not_contain hits without adding the positive procedural literals leaves the file describing nothing — Dev must add new wording.

files_and_patterns.must_not_contain_rationale: current qa.md:995 ends with 'run `pytest tests/generated/ -q`' (unscoped — codex F4 anti-pattern); current qa.md:994 begins with 'Read `tests/generated/manifest.json`. For every `active_tests[]` entry' (root-iteration — codex F4 anti-pattern). Both literals match TODAY so the check fails today.
files_and_patterns.must_contain_all_rationale: binds Dev to codex F4 (scoping pytest to tests/generated/<task_id>/) + codex F6 (schema fields root_index_path, per_task_manifest_path, task_id in manifest_verification block) + the F4 intent literal ('current task_id') that proves Dev rewrote Phase 5 to scope to the current task.

must_not_contain: verify on disk that `tests/generated/manifest.json` AND `docs/dev/test-writer-report-<task_id>.json` BOTH exist
files_and_patterns.must_not_contain_rationale: current commands/dev.md:632 verifies only two artifacts (root manifest + report); per Section 7 P1.2 lockstep and codex F5, the dispatch must require all three artifacts after the manifest contract is Option (b). The literal is verbatim from the current file so the check fails today.
files_and_patterns.must_contain_all_rationale: forces Dev to add the per-task manifest path to the verification AND a phrase indicating three artifacts (Section 7 lockstep). 'all three' is the natural English wording for the new check (codex F5).

items.ac_uid_regenerated_reason: OBJ-1 + OBJ-2 + codex finding 3 iter2 — check object now uses must_not_contain (negative checks against current drifted wording) + must_contain_all (positive checks for new wording), and adds commands/dev.md sub-check for Section 7 P1.2 lockstep

items.when: the file is read AND git check-ignore is invoked on three runtime artifact paths from the project root
items.then: .gitignore contains the three literal lines '/agent-scores.json', '/agent-scores.json.lock', '/.claude/specs/spec-*/'; `git check-ignore agent-scores.json` exits 0; `git check-ignore agent-scores.json.lock` exits 0; `git check-ignore .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/cp-state-ba.json` exits 0

args: set -e; grep -qE '^/agent-scores\.json$' .gitignore && grep -qE '^/agent-scores\.json\.lock$' .gitignore && grep -qE '^/\.claude/specs/spec-\*/$' .gitignore && git check-ignore agent-scores.json && git check-ignore agent-scores.json.lock && git check-ignore .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/cp-state-ba.json

items.given: README.md, INDEX.md, and .claude/INDEX.md after Cycle 2 dev edits
items.when: each index file is grepped for the literal strings 'agent-scores.json' and 'agent-scores.json.lock'
items.then: neither string appears in any of the three index files; entries previously at README.md:16-17 and the INDEX.md leaked lines are gone (codex F7: .gitignore is appended BEFORE regen, so the regenerator skips the now-ignored files)

items.given: scripts/score-inject.sh, scripts/score-update.sh, scripts/canary-verify.sh after Cycle 2 dev edits
items.when: each python3 invocation site is inspected for the venv-activation prefix
items.then: all 5 sites have `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate &&` (or equivalent `source ${HOME}/.claude/venv/bin/activate &&`) preceding the python3 invocation, with the && ordering preserved so pipe stdin still flows into python3 (codex F8: canary's `echo "$payload" | source ... && python3 hook` would feed the payload into `source`; correct form is `source ... && echo "$payload" | python3 hook`)

args: set -e; grep -qE 'source\s+(\$\{?HOME\}?|~)/.claude/venv/bin/activate\s+&&\s+python3' scripts/score-inject.sh && grep -qE 'source\s+(\$\{?HOME\}?|~)/.claude/venv/bin/activate\s+&&\s+python3' scripts/score-update.sh && [ "$(grep -cE 'source\s+(\$\{?HOME\}?|~)/.claude/venv/bin/activate\s+&&' scripts/canary-verify.sh)" -ge 3 ]

items.when: the file is grepped for the literal strings 'Sub-step 12.1' and the cross-reference at the former line ~1110
items.then: the literal string 'Sub-step 12.1' does NOT appear anywhere in commands/dev.md; the new heading uses an integer-step-compliant form (e.g. 'Step 12a' — per Standard 4 integer-step-numbering rule, decimal step numbers are forbidden); the cross-reference at the former line ~1110 now matches (no orphaned reference to 'Sub-step 12.1')

items.when: the file is grepped for hardcoded '/tmp/' string literals (excluding comments)
items.then: no occurrences of `/tmp/canary-` literals remain; the script uses `mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/canary-verify.XXXXXX"` (codex F9: ad-hoc ${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/fixed-name still has collision and cleanup risk) or equivalent and registers an EXIT trap (`trap 'rm -rf "$CANARY_TMPDIR"' EXIT` or equivalent cleanup) so the temp directory is removed on exit

args: set -e; ! grep -qE '"/tmp/canary-' scripts/canary-verify.sh && grep -qE 'mktemp\s+-d' scripts/canary-verify.sh && grep -qE 'trap.+EXIT' scripts/canary-verify.sh

items.when: the Standard 6 Exemption block is read
items.then: a SECOND exemption paragraph follows the existing 'verbatim user-binding quotes' paragraph; the new exemption is NARROW (codex F10): it covers ONLY user-facing source-language strings authored in the spec's source language (rank names, AskUserQuestion choice labels, prompt-injection tail phrases) in spec-authored UI surfaces; it explicitly states that code/script comments, diagnostic stderr messages, command prose, and implementation notes remain English; it cites the originating task-id (20260519-211515) and the spec (spec-20260518-225715) so future readers can trace the decision

args: set -e; grep -qE 'spec-20260518-225715' agents/style-inspector.md && grep -qE '20260519-211515' agents/style-inspector.md && grep -qE 'source.language|source-language' agents/style-inspector.md

codex_consult_iter2.feedback_summary: Codex sustained all 3 BA-proposed repairs as on-direction but flagged 4 follow-up findings: (1) test-writer.md positive checks gameable by deletion-without-replacement; (2) qa.md missing must_not_contain for codex F4 root-iteration anti-pattern; (3) commands/dev.md verification step needs lockstep enforcement per Section 7 P1.2; (4) AC-C2-01 should use set_equals not contains_all to enforce Section 7 'limited to'. All 4 findings have PROPOSED_FIXes and have been applied.

findings.issue: OBJ-1 test-writer.md positive checks still satisfied by existing schema block (80-127); Dev could delete must_not_contain strings without adding new Procedure wording
findings.proposed_fix: Add 'per-task manifest' + 'root index' to must_contain_all; add 'manifest.json `active_tests[].hook_check`' (line 216) to must_not_contain
findings.rationale: Codex correctly identified that the OBJ-1 repair was necessary but insufficient. Without positive procedural literals, Dev could mechanically delete drifted sentences. With 'per-task manifest' + 'root index' literals required, Dev must add new procedural wording. The line 216 checklist drift is also a genuine drift point that should be included.

findings.issue: OBJ-2 qa.md repair forbids only the unscoped pytest line (995), not the root-iteration anti-pattern (994: 'Read tests/generated/manifest.json. For every active_tests[] entry')
findings.proposed_fix: Add 'Read `tests/generated/manifest.json`. For every `active_tests[]` entry' to must_not_contain
findings.rationale: Codex F4 is about scoping to current task_id. The root-iteration form is the anti-pattern this fix is targeting; Dev could leave it intact and only add scoped pytest below it, partially passing. Forbidding the root-iteration form forces Dev to restructure Phase 5.

findings.issue: Section 7 P1.2 says 'Update test-writer.md AND qa.md AND commands/dev.md verification step in lockstep' but AC-C2-02 commands/dev.md sub-check only requires manifest path strings be present, not that the verification step requires all three artifacts
findings.proposed_fix: Add must_not_contain on current line 632 wording ('verify on disk that `tests/generated/manifest.json` AND `docs/dev/test-writer-report-<task_id>.json` BOTH exist') + must_contain_all on three-artifact form including 'all three'
findings.rationale: Section 7 P1.2 explicitly requires lockstep across all three files. Without this sub-check, commands/dev.md verification could be left unchanged and Section 7 P1.2 would be partially satisfied. The must_not_contain literal is verbatim from current commands/dev.md:632.

findings.issue: AC-C2-01 allowed_write_path_prefixes uses match=contains_all, which would allow Dev to add stream stubs/tmp/other unrelated entries; Section 7 P1.1 says 'Write/Edit limited to' which means equality, not superset
findings.proposed_fix: Change match from contains_all to set_equals
findings.rationale: set_equals enforces the 'limited to' wording. Combined with the prose tightening (path (a)), the AC now consistently asserts the 5-entry exact list.

findings.proposed_fix: Concurrence with BA path (a) decision (Section 7 verbatim)
findings.rationale: Codex confirmed path (a) is correct per Section 7 P1.1 verbatim; no further action needed beyond prose tightening + set_equals.

codex_consult_iter2.feedback_incorporated: All 4 codex PROPOSED_FIX findings applied to AC-C2-01 and AC-C2-02. ac_uid regenerated for both. Observation-only finding 5 logged. Cycle 1 ACs (AC-C2-03 through AC-C2-08) unchanged from iter1.
